commands.review_summary:
The PR routes inbound document extraction through existing attachment and PDF limits, replaces large data-URL regular-expression parsing with iterative parsing, and removes stale stack-overflow queue entries.

PR surface: Source +128, Tests +307, Docs 0. Total +435 across 18 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source and report evidence: current main uses full-payload regular expressions in the affected gateway and Control UI paths, and the linked issue records the resulting stack-overflow failures. This review did not independently execute a large upload against current main.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Require contributor end-to-end proof and a maintainer decision on the upgrade-visible extraction-budget semantics; no confirmed mechanical patch defect currently warrants an automated repair.

target.title: Fix stack-safe large attachment handling
target.body:
## Summary

This is the canonical-source replacement for the closed #90111 approach, following amknight's review: it uses the existing media-understanding/file-extraction pipeline instead of adding a parallel inbound-PDF prompt-context subsystem.

- Route inbound file/PDF extraction limits through existing config keys: `agents.defaults.mediaMaxMb`, `agents.defaults.pdfMaxBytesMb`, and `agents.defaults.pdfMaxPages`.
- Keep explicit `gateway.http.endpoints.responses.files` limits authoritative.
- Move `resolveChatAttachmentMaxBytes` into shared media config so the gateway accept path and media-understanding extraction path share the same cap.
- Replace full-string data URL/base64 regex parsing in gateway and Control UI paths with stack-safe index/iterative parsing.
- Drop stale Control UI queue items failed by the old `Maximum call stack size exceeded` bug.

Closes #90098.

## Deliberately Not Included

- No new `inbound-pdf-context` subsystem, `MediaExtractedContext`, or prompt-string scanning.
- No `chat.send` oversized managed-PDF pass-through. That is a separate sandbox/staging-boundary question and is not needed for the canonical extraction-limit fix.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/media-understanding/apply.file-limits.test.ts src/media-understanding/apply.test.ts src/gateway/chat-attachments.test.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts ui/src/ui/chat/composer-persistence.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts` - 430 passed across 4 shards
- `pnpm tsgo:core`
- `pnpm tsgo:test:src`
- `pnpm tsgo:test:ui`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 ...` on the 18 touched files
- `pnpm lint`
- `git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD`

## Real Behavior Proof

- **Behavior addressed:** Large inbound PDFs/files accepted by chat attachment handling no longer degrade to marker-only context because media-understanding now resolves inbound file limits from the same existing agent defaults. Large Control UI/gateway data URLs no longer rely on full-string regex captures that can throw `Maximum call stack size exceeded`. Stale failed queue entries from the old parser are dropped.

- **Real environment tested:** Live installed OpenClaw runtime on Joey's VPS, using `/home/exedev/.npm-global/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist` and the local post-update Hook 46 replacement under `/home/exedev/.openclaw/workspace/scripts/patch-pdf-media-runtime.py`.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  - `python3 scripts/patch-pdf-media-runtime.py`
  - `OPENCLAW_HOLD_GATEWAY=1 scripts/post-update-hook.sh`
  - `rg -n "openclaw-patch-pdf-media-runtime:(canonical-limits-v2|attachments-v2|control-ui-v2)" /home/exedev/.npm-global/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/apply-*.js /home/exedev/.npm-global/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/attachment-normalize-*.js /home/exedev/.npm-global/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/control-ui/assets/index-jM0oJkaS-pdfq3.js`
  - `rg -n "openclaw-patch-pdf-media-runtime:(autoreply-v1|embedded-v1|managed-pass-v1)|applyInboundPdfContextIfNeeded|buildInboundPdfExtractedContext|PDF attachment text extracted from inbound media|inbound PDF context" /home/exedev/.npm-global/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/get-reply-*.js /home/exedev/.npm-global/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/selection-*.js /home/exedev/.npm-global/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/chat-*.js`
  - `node --check` on the live patched `apply-*`, `attachment-normalize-*`, active Control UI asset, restored `get-reply-*`, and restored `chat-*` bundles
  - `systemctl --failed --no-pager` and `systemctl is-active openclaw stackone-defender subconscious-loop`

- **Evidence after fix:** Copied live terminal output from the installed runtime patch and post-update hook:

```text
[pdf-media-runtime] cleanup: restored get-reply-BpFiu3Nn.js from get-reply-BpFiu3Nn.js.bak-pdf-media-runtime-20260609T184101Z
[pdf-media-runtime] cleanup: restored chat-DOMgZOP2.js from chat-DOMgZOP2.js.bak-pdf-media-runtime-20260609T184101Z
[pdf-media-runtime] canonical-limits: patched apply-2JWnPrPO.js
[pdf-media-runtime] attachments: patched attachment-normalize-BvtU5UAO.js
[pdf-media-runtime] control-ui: wrote cache-busted asset index-jM0oJkaS-pdfq2.js
[pdf-media-runtime] control-ui: updated index.html -> index-jM0oJkaS-pdfq2.js
[pdf-media-runtime] completed

[Hook 46] PDF/media canonical limits + stack-safe upload handling
       [pdf-media-runtime] cleanup: changed 0 file(s)
       [pdf-media-runtime] canonical-limits: marker present in apply-2JWnPrPO.js
       [pdf-media-runtime] attachments: marker present in attachment-normalize-BvtU5UAO.js
       [pdf-media-runtime] control-ui: marker present in index-jM0oJkaS-pdfq3.js
       [pdf-media-runtime] completed

/home/exedev/.npm-global/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/apply-2JWnPrPO.js:116:// openclaw-patch-pdf-media-runtime:canonical-limits-v2
/home/exedev/.npm-global/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/attachment-normalize-BvtU5UAO.js:1:// openclaw-patch-pdf-media-runtime:attachments-v2
/home/exedev/.npm-global/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/control-ui/assets/index-jM0oJkaS-pdfq3.js:1:// openclaw-patch-pdf-media-runtime:control-ui-v2

Rejected v1 helper scan returned no matches in active get-reply/selection/chat bundles.
node --check passed on the patched/restored live bundles.
systemctl --failed: 0 loaded units listed.
openclaw: active
stackone-defender: active
subconscious-loop: active
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The live installed runtime now has canonical-limit v2, attachment parser v2, and Control UI v2 markers; the rejected v1 `applyInboundPdfContextIfNeeded`/`inbound PDF context` helper is absent from active reply/chat bundles; the active Control UI HTML points at a new cache-busted asset; syntax checks pass; OpenClaw remains active with no failed units.

- **What was not tested:** A fresh manual browser upload against the production Control UI was not rerun in this session. The live installed runtime patch was applied and verified, and the source branch has focused behavior tests for >5MB PDF extraction, over-cap skip behavior, `pdfMaxPages` routing, large data URL parsing, pasted-image parsing, and stale queue cleanup.

Generated with Claude Code.
target.author: joeykrug
